page: Microsoft Azure D8d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 8 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor runs at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz and is equipped with a 48 MB L3 cache. It includes hardware acceleration flags such as AVX-512 and AVX-512 VNNI for vector calculations, alongside cryptographic extensions like AES and SHA_NI.

The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per virtual CPU. It also includes a bundled 307 GB local SSD storage drive, offering fast temporary storage directly on the host. In benchmark evaluations, the D8d_v5 demonstrates consistent mid-tier performance. Its memory subsystem achieves a read-only cached bandwidth of 417.21 GB/sec and an uncached large-block write speed of 41.67 GB/sec. CPU performance is characterized by a PassMark CPU score of 11469.91 and a single-core Geekbench score of 1653.0, positioning it firmly within the average performance tier for its hardware class.

While multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance registers in the lower tier at 32.04 GB/sec, other multi-threaded workloads like software compilation and image processing maintain average throughput. The instance handles database operations efficiently, registering 4094.03 operations per second in PassMark database tests and 8.08 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Static web serving throughput reaches 12.33 GB/sec. These characteristics make the D8d_v5 highly cost-efficient for balanced workloads requiring local scratch space, such as relational databases, web serving, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: Microsoft Azure D8d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 307 GB local SSD. The instance delivers balanced, mid-tier performance across key benchmarks, including a PassMark CPU score of 11469.91 and a Redis SET throughput of 8.08 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth reaches 417.21 GB/sec for cached reads, while static web serving throughput averages 12.33 GB/sec. Lacking a GPU, it relies on CPU instruction sets like AVX-512 for vector workloads. The D8d_v5 is a cost-effective option for workloads requiring a balance of compute, memory, and fast local temporary storage, making it well-suited for web servers, database hosting, and general-purpose application environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8d_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 307 GB local SSD storage, delivering balanced mid-tier benchmark performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8d_v5 is a general-purpose instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated local SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB RAM
Includes 307 GB of bundled local SSD storage for fast temporary data access
Features AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration flags
Delivers balanced mid-tier performance in database and web serving benchmarks
Best suited for relational databases, web servers, and general-purpose applications
